Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results and Accession numbers list ISA1 as At2g39930.

Applied_Species.reason: The heterologous system is yeast throughout the paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all used in the Results to assess ISA effects: zymogram, combinatorial yeast strains, glucan fractionation/quantification, iodine staining, microscopy, and CLD analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported: ISA1 acts with ISA2, ISA is the major driver of insoluble glucan formation, and ISA-expressing lines accumulate linear malto-oligosaccharides.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says ISA1 and ISA2 together form the active heteromultimer and notes rare ISA-independent insoluble glucan formation in some BE3-only combinations.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolative application statement not directly tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper supports that SS1 promotes soluble glucans and decreases insoluble glucans; reduced crystallinity is discussed as a probable consequence.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly reports improved SS1-dependent glucan synthesis when endogenous glycogenins are present.
